name: Vegetable Beef Stew
recipeIngredient: 1 4 pound beef round or chuck steak, 1 1/2 inch thick, cut into 1 1/2-inch cubes
3 carrots, peeled, split lengthwise, cut in half
1 10 oz pkg frozen peas, corn, green beans, or lima beans -- partially thawed
1/2 cup dry red wine, or water
1 14.5 oz can tomato wedges or slices, drained, optional
recipeInstructions:
1. If the beef is extra lean, thoroughly wipe the cubed beef on absorbent towels to dry.
2. If the meat contains fat, quickly brown it in a large skillet to sear and remove the fat; drain well.
3. Place the beef cubes in a Crock-Pot.
4. Combine 1/3 cup flour with the salt and pepper; toss with beef to coat thoroughly.
5. Add all vegetables except tomato wedges to the Crock-Pot and mix well.
6. Combine beef broth, wine, sugar, and Kitchen Bouquet. Pour over meat and vegetables; stir carefully.
7. Add drained tomatoes and stir well. Cover and cook on Low for 10 to 14 hours or on High for 4 to 5 1/2 hours.
8. One hour before serving, turn the setting to High. Make a smooth paste of 1/4 cup flour and the water; stir into the Crock-Pot. Cover and cook until thickened.
9. Serves 8 to 10
Note: For better color, add half of the frozen vegetables at the beginning; add the remaining half during the last hour.
